text:
Market today: Today's vibe is strong for tech and chips, with big news from Apple's Broadcom deal, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 approval, and a new Nvidia challenger getting huge funding. Geopolitical noise is in the background, but the clear drivers are in your core sectors.

Your holdings: SOXL is down big at -35.06% and flagged "SELL," a major concern for a leveraged position. SPAL, a brand-new and very volatile leveraged ETF, is already underwater at -23.1% and below its stop. QQQI is flagged "TRIM" at -2.16% and nearing its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your leveraged positions, SOXL and SPAL, for any further downside. Also, watch for continued strength in the broader chip sector (SMH) given today's positive news.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your volatile, leveraged holdings while leveraging strength in core tech/chip names.
plays: Sell SPAL immediately; it's a new, leveraged position already -23.1% and below its 21.38 stop.
Trim QQQI at 55.19 to honor the "TRIM" status and manage risk before it hits its 52 stop.
Consider AMAT (entry 547.11-545.21, stop 541.98) on the back of Apple's Broadcom commitment, as a chip equipment play.
